rationale: The citing paper uses Aribarg and Schwartz (2020) to support a specific conceptual claim: ad-content congruency in digital environments improves consumer responses. That claim is then carried into the citing paper's theory development, where fit between unsafe adjacent content and brand image is proposed and tested as a moderator of brand safety effects. This is more than a passing literature-list mention because the cited finding helps structure the conceptual framework and empirical tests. The citing paper does not reuse the cited paper's methods, but it relies on its insight as prior grounding.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Aribarg and Schwartz (2020) as prior empirical support that ad-content congruency matters in digital advertising. It builds its brand-safety framework on that premise by theorizing and testing how brand-content fit moderates negative adjacency effects.
